one_line: A senior Ainnurruvar (Manigramam) guild merchant sailing Chola-era trade routes across the Indian Ocean in the 11th–12th centuries.

appearance.age_look: Early 40s, lean but sturdy build, weathered from sea travel.
appearance.face: Oval face with medium-brown Tamil complexion, sharp nose, neatly trimmed short beard and moustache, slight crow’s-feet at the eyes, calm but keen gaze, default expression thoughtful with a subtle confident half-smile.
appearance.hair: Thick black hair, oiled and parted in the middle, pulled back into a short low knot at the nape; a simple off-white cotton turban strip wrapped once around the head and tied at the back, leaving the knot visible.
appearance.costume: A knee-length maroon cotton angavastram draped over the left shoulder and across the chest, leaving right arm mostly free, worn over a fitted, short-sleeved off-white cotton tunic; lower garment is a tightly wrapped cream-coloured veshti (dhoti) in Tamil style reaching mid-calf, front pleats neat, bare feet in simple dark brown leather sandals appropriate for port travel; a narrow dark indigo cotton waist-sash tied at the right hip; modest gold stud earrings, a thin gold chain with a small, flat rectangular pendant at the chest, and a set of three dark wooden prayer beads on the left wrist.
appearance.signature: A medium-sized, slightly curved iron merchant’s scale balance held in his right hand, with a worn dark-wood beam, bronze pans hanging from thin chains, and a rolled palm-leaf account ledger tucked under his left arm with visible cord binding.

ref_desc: A lean Tamil merchant in his early 40s with an oval medium-brown face, short beard, low-knot black hair and simple off-white turban strip, wearing maroon angavastram, off-white tunic, cream veshti, dark indigo waist-sash, leather sandals, modest gold and bead jewellery, carrying a curved iron merchant’s scale and palm-leaf ledger in maroon, off-white, cream, dark indigo and dark brown tones.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of a senior Chola-era Ainnurruvar sea-merchant: a lean Tamil man in his early 40s with an oval medium-brown face, sharp nose, short beard and moustache, calm keen eyes and a thoughtful half-smile, his thick black hair oiled and parted, pulled into a short low knot at the nape with a simple off-white cotton turban strip wrapped once around his head and tied at the back. He wears a knee-length maroon cotton angavastram draped over his left shoulder and across his chest, over a fitted short-sleeved off-white cotton tunic. His lower garment is a tightly wrapped cream-coloured veshti (dhoti) in Tamil style with neat front pleats, reaching mid-calf, and he stands in simple dark brown leather sandals suitable for port travel. Around his waist is a narrow dark indigo cotton sash tied at the right hip; his jewellery is modest—small gold stud earrings, a thin gold chain with a small flat rectangular pendant at his chest, and three dark wooden prayer beads on his left wrist. In his right hand he prominently holds a medium-sized slightly curved iron merchant’s scale with a dark-wood beam and bronze pans on chains, while under his left arm he tucks a rolled palm-leaf account ledger bound with cord. Use clear, consistent maroon, off-white, cream, dark indigo and dark brown tones, and present him front-facing with a crisp, readable design that can be redrawn identically across many panels.
